facts.text: Balmville once housed the Balmville Tree, the oldest Eastern cottonwood on record, standing over 15 feet tall and 25 feet wide before it was removed in 2015.
The Balmville Tree earned a place on the National Register of Historic Places in 2000, honoring its role in American history.
Balmville sits on the southeastern edge of the Town of Newburgh, overlooking the Hudson River with pristine views that attract affluent residents.
Historic streets like River Road and Grand Avenue once hosted a community of carpenters, smiths, and vintners, reflecting Balmville’s early rural character.
In 2000, the New York State Department of Parks, Recreation & Historic Preservation nominated the Bloomer-Dailey House and Balmville Tree for the National Register, spotlighting local heritage.
Newburgh’s Wheelmen’s Club rode to the grand old tree each week, making Balmville a favored stop for early bicycle enthusiasts.
